title: Muninn derived document frontmatter (summaries/, entities/, concepts/, questions/, insights/)
description: The frontmatter contract of a derived document (SPEC §5.2, format 1.0). Provenance is required (I2) and may name ONLY sources (I3 — the no-chaining invariant); each entry records the source's content hash at derivation time, which is exactly what makes staleness detectable (I5). Unknown fields are permitted (§5). What this schema cannot check — that every provenance id resolves to a real source and not a derived doc (L2/L3), hash currency (L4), self_hash integrity (L19) — is enforced by the linter.

id.description: Stable id (§4.1). Recommended prefixes: sum-, ent-, con-, q-, ins-.

abstract.description: One skim line, projected into index.md and project pages (§5.3). Rides this doc's provenance — never a separate derivation.

sources.description: Provenance (I2): an unordered set of source peers, never other derived docs (I3).

id.description: A SOURCE id — a derived-doc id here is summary chaining (L2).

hash.description: The source's content hash at derivation time; staleness (I5) is exactly this hash no longer matching.

spans.description: Where in the source (optional but encouraged).

see_also.description: Navigation links to OTHER derived docs — links, never provenance (I3).

derivation.description: HOW this doc was grounded (ADR-0011); absent defaults to extracted. The assurance rung `ask` rolls up weakest-link.

as_of.description: The date a TIME-RELATIVE claim was true (ADR-0034); surfaced on-load, never by lint.

connectors.description: Connectors a landscape doc ASSERTS but hasn't ingested from (ADR-0028, T-070).

self_hash.description: Core-stamped hash over the authored content (title + abstract + body, ADR-0029); enforcement is the opt-in L19.
